executive_summary: Your supply chain is currently stable across all three risk dimensions, but the dashboard reveals a concentrated geopolitical vulnerability: nine of your flagged suppliers cluster around two fault lines—US-China trade tensions (affecting five critical and high-exposure suppliers in EMS, batteries, and nicotine) and energy/infrastructure stress in emerging markets (Sappi in South Africa, ITC in India). The China-exposed cohort (Huizhou BYD, Smoore, EVE Energy, Porton, IP Sun) warrants priority because they carry either critical or high BAT exposure and face overlapping Taiwan and export-control risks; this isn't five separate problems but one systemic pressure point. Stora Enso and Tae Young present genuine but lower-urgency tails: NATO border proximity and North Korea proximity respectively are real, but neither carries critical BAT exposure and both have structural stability otherwise. No immediate action required given the GREEN rating, but use the next 60 days to audit dual-sourcing readiness for your China EMS and battery tiers—escalation speed would matter if US-Taiwan tensions sharpen.
